volumeTitle: The Little Carthaginian. Pseudolus. The Rope
label: The Rope (Section 5.3.1367 - Section 5.3.1414)

sections.label: Section 5.3.1367
sections.original: Immo hercle , ut scias gaudere me , mihi triobolum
sections.translation: Aye , by my faith , that you may be sure that I ’ m rejoiced , you shan ’ t give me three obols for her ; I excuse you .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1368b
sections.translation: I ’ faith , you are acting kindly .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1369
sections.translation: No , troth ; it ’ s really yourself , indeed , that ’ s doing so .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1369b
sections.translation: Hark you , you ’ ve got the wallet now .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1370
sections.translation: I have got it .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1370b
sections.translation: Make haste .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1370c
sections.translation: Make haste about what ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.1370d
sections.translation: To pay me the money .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1371
sections.original: Neque edepol tibi do , neque quicquam debeo .
sections.translation: By my troth , I ’ ll neither give you anything nor do I owe you anything .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1371b
sections.translation: What mode of proceeding is this ? Don ’ t you owe it me ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.1372b
sections.translation: Troth , not I indeed .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1372c
sections.translation: Didn ’ t you promise it me upon your oath ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.1373
sections.original: Iuratus sum , et nunc iurabo , si quid vóluptati est mihi :
sections.translation: I did take an oath , and now I ’ ll take an oath , if it is in any way my own pleasure ; oaths were invented for preserving property , not for losing it .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1375
sections.original: Cedo sis mihi talentum magnum argenti , periurissime .
sections.translation: Give me , will you , a great talent of silver , you most perjured fellow .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1376
sections.original: Gripe , quod tu istum talentum poscis ?
sections.translation: Gripus , what talent is it you are asking him for ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.1376b
sections.translation: He promised it me on oath .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1377b
sections.original: Libet iurare . tun meo póntifex peiurio es ?
sections.translation: I chose to swear ; ( turning to DAEMONES ) are you the priest [*] as to my perjury ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.1378
sections.original: Qua pro re argentum promisit hic tibi ?
sections.translation: For what reason did he promise you the money ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.1378b
sections.translation: If I restored this wallet into his hands , he swore that he would give

sections.label: Section 5.3.1380
sections.translation: me a great talent of silver .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1380b
sections.translation: Find me a person with whom I may go to the judge , to decide whether you did not make the bargain with wicked fraudulence , and whether I am yet five-and-twenty years old [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1382b
sections.translation: Go to the judge with him .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1382c
sections.translation: No ; I must have some other person .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1383
sections.original: Iám ab isto auferre haud potis sim , si istunc condemnavero .
sections.translation: Then I shan ’ t allow you to take it away from him , unless I shall have found him guilty . Did you promise him the money ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.1384b
sections.translation: I confess it .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1384c
sections.translation: What you promised my slave

sections.label: Section 5.3.1385
sections.original: promisisti , meum esse oportet , ne tu , leno , postules
sections.translation: must needs be my own . Don ’ t you be supposing , Procurer , that you are to be using your pimping honesty here . That can ’ t be .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1386b
sections.translation: Did you fancy now that you had got hold of a person whom you might cheat ? It must be paid down here ( holding his hand ) , good silver coin ; I shall , at once , pay it to him ( pointing to DAEMONES ) , so that he may give me my liberty .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1389
sections.original: Quando ergo erga te benignus ego fui atque opera mea
sections.translation: Inasmuch , therefore , as I have acted courteously towards you , and by my means these things ( pointing to the wallet ) have been saved for you —

sections.label: Section 5.3.1390b
sections.original: Immo hercle mea , ne tu dicas tua .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , by my means , rather ; don ’ t say by yours .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1391
sections.original: ( Si sapies , tacebis ) tum te mihi benigne itidem addecet
sections.translation: If you are prudent you ’ ll hold your tongue . ( To LABRAX .) Then it befits you in a like courteous manner kindly to return the obligation to myself , who so well merit the same .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1392b
sections.translation: You are pleading , of course , for my right ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.1393b
sections.original: Mirum quin tuom ius meo periclo aps te expetam .
sections.translation: It would be a wonder if I didn ’ t , at a loss to myself , ask you to forego your right .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1394
sections.original: Salvos sum , leno labascit , libertas portenditur .
sections.translation: I ’ m all right ; the Procurer ’ s giving way ; my freedom is at hand .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1395
sections.original: Vidulum istunc ille invenit , illud mancipium meum est ;
sections.translation: He found this wallet ; he is my slave . I therefore have preserved this for you , together with a large sum of money .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1397
sections.original: Gratiam habeo , et de talento nulla causa est quin feras ,
sections.translation: I return you thanks , and with regard to the talent that I promised on oath to him , there ’ s no reason that you shouldn ’ t receive it .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1398b
sections.original: Heus tu , mihi dato ergo , si sapis .
sections.translation: Hark you , give it me then , if you are wise .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1399
sections.translation: Will you hold your tongue , or not ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.1399b
sections.original: Tu meam rem simulas agere , tibi mu ⋯ .
sections.translation: You pretend to be acting on my side : I tell you ⋯

sections.label: Section 5.3.1400
sections.original: non hercle istoc me intervortes , si aliam praedam perdidi .
sections.translation: by my troth , you shan ’ t do me out of that , if I did lose the other booty [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1401
sections.original: Vapulabis , verbum si addis istúc unum .
sections.translation: You shall have a beating if you add a single word .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1401b
sections.translation: Troth now , do you kill me even ; I ’ ll never be silent on any terms , unless my mouth is shut with the talent .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1403
sections.original: Tibi operam hic quidem dát . tace .
sections.translation: For yourself , in fact , is he using his exertions ; do hold your tongue .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1403b
sections.translation: Step this way , Procurer .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1403c
sections.translation: Very well .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1404
sections.original: Palam age , nolo ego murmurillum neque susurrum fieri .
sections.translation: Proceed openly ; I don ’ t want any whisperings or mumblings to be going on .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1405
sections.original: Dic mihi , quanti íllam emisti tuam alteram mulierculam ,
sections.translation: Tell me , at what price did you buy that other young woman , Ampelisca ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.1406b
sections.translation: I paid down a thousand didrachms .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1406c
sections.translation: Should you like me to make you a handsome offer ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.1407b
sections.translation: I should like it much .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1408
sections.translation: I ’ ll divide the talent .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1408b
sections.translation: You act fairly .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1408c
sections.translation: For that other woman Ampelisca , that she may be free , take you one half , and give the other half to him .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1410
sections.translation: By all means .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1410b
sections.original: Pro illo dimidio égo Gripum emittam manu ,
sections.translation: For that half I ’ ll give his freedom to Gripus , by means of whom you found your wallet , and I my daughter .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1411b
sections.translation: You act fairly ; I return you many thanks .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1412b
sections.original: Quam mox mi argentum ergo redditur ?
sections.translation: How soon then is the money to be returned to me ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.1413
sections.original: Res soluta est , Gripe . ego habeo .
sections.translation: The money ’ s paid , Gripus ; I ’ ve got it .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1413b
sections.translation: You , faith ; but I had rather it were myself .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1414
sections.original: Nihil hercle hic tibi est , né tu speres . iuris iurandi volo
sections.translation: I ’ faith , there ’ s nothing for you here , so don ’ t you be expecting it . I wish you to release him from his oath .
